title: Vegan brownies
subtitle: Dense, fudgy chocolate brownies with no eggs or dairy
excerpt: Fudgy vegan brownies that achieve the same dense, crackled result as the butter-egg original through coconut oil, dark chocolate, and a high sugar ratio. No aquafaba, no flax eggs.
sourceNotes: Fudgy vegan brownies rely on the same principle as the original: a high fat and sugar ratio relative to flour, and a chocolate base that sets as it cools. Without eggs to provide structure, the recipe requires a slightly higher flour ratio and relies more heavily on melted chocolate (rather than cocoa powder alone) to set the centre. This method is adapted from approaches developed in Isa Chandra Moskowitz's Veganomicon (2007) and Meera Sodha's East (2019).

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Cut into squares and freeze in a sealed bag for up to 3 months. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best the day after baking. Store in an airtight tin at room temperature for up to 4 days.

content.text: The batter will look very liquid going into the tin. That is correct. The melted chocolate sets during baking and creates the fudgy structure without eggs. The high caster sugar ratio is not a mistake; the sugar is what creates the crackled, slightly glossy top.

Use dairy-free dark chocolate with at least 70% cocoa. Lower-percentage chocolate contains more sugar and milk solids; it will make a sweeter, softer brownie that may not set properly. Check the label for milk derivatives if strict vegan compliance is needed.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease and line a 20 x 20 cm square tin. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{coconut-oil}} together in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water. Stir until smooth and remove from the heat. Stir in the {{caster-sugar}}, {{oat-milk}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until just combined. Fold in the {{chocolate-chips}} if using.

Pour into the tin and spread level. Bake for 25 to 28 minutes until the surface is crackled and set and the edges have begun to pull away from the tin. A skewer inserted in the centre should come out with moist crumbs, not wet batter. Cool completely in the tin before cutting (at least 2 hours). The brownies firm significantly as they cool.
